reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Friday 15 May
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Saturday 9 May to Friday 15 May.
reports.summary: The week from Saturday 9 May to Friday 15 May shifted from gas-heavy weekdays into a cleaner, wind-led weekend. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Friday 15 May, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 58.7% of measured generation across settlement periods. Gas was highest on Monday 11 May and lowest on Friday 15 May.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Thursday 14 May
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Thursday 14 May, measured average generation was about 11.9 GW. Renewables averaged 47.9% of measured generation, gas averaged 3.0 GW, wind averaged 3.8 GW and solar averaged 1.8 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 58.7% average renewable share on Friday 15 May, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 4.8 GW average wind output on Tuesday 12 May, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 1.9 GW average solar output on Saturday 9 May, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 3.3 GW average gas output on Monday 11 May, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 58.7% on Friday 15 May
• Highest average wind output: ~4.8 GW on Tuesday 12 May
• Highest average solar output: ~1.9 GW on Saturday 9 May
• Highest average gas output: ~3.3 GW on Monday 11 May

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-05-15

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
